recordings.title: Missed Reset After Mid Inhibitor and Shutdown Protection Leak
recordings.feedbackTitle: Missed Reset After Mid Inhibitor and Shutdown Protection Leak
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You continued farming and moving mid after taking the inhibitor, without resetting for tempo or recalling after a big shutdown, leaving yourself exposed and desynced from your team. Fix: After shutting down a key enemy or taking mid inhibitor, use the recall and reset window to spend gold and sync up with your team for the next play instead of aimless mid waves.
recordings.gameDetail: Around 5:27, Samira and an ally near tower on bot lane, enemy with spell shield; this is the start or nearest visible start of the main mistake window. Mistake: You continued farming and moving mid after taking the inhibitor, without resetting for tempo or recalling after a big shutdown, leaving yourself exposed and desynced from your team. Fix: After shutting down a key enemy or taking mid inhibitor, use the recall and reset window to spend gold and sync up with your team for the next play instead of aimless mid waves. Conversion means turning a fight win or gold lead into something that remains after the fight ends: tower damage, base damage, dragon/Baron setup, or a safe reset with spent gold. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.
recordings.eventEvidence: 5:27 shows Samira and an ally near tower on bot lane, enemy with spell shield.
recordings.goodThing: You did correctly group and play for mid inhibitor with the team after the outer towers were down.
recordings.whyTrust: This advice is universal at all ranks and unlocks actual team play and baron/dragon setups, instead of risking a 1v4 collapse where you give away shutdown gold and lose tempo.
recordings.evidence: 5:27 Samira and an ally near tower on bot lane, enemy with spell shield
recordings.pattern: You mechanically play fights well but default to continued farming or mid waves after a big structure, instead of using that to synchronize resets and set up grouped plays with your fresh power spike.
recordings.diamondRule: After mid inhibitor or shutdown streak, recall with the team as soon as the map is open and you finish the fight, unless there's a free Baron/Dragon immediately.
recordings.drill: Next game, when your team gets an inhibitor, immediately ping recall and reset, then group for Baron or Dragon after spending.

recordings.eventEvidence: 1:29 shows Samira and allies fight enemy bot lane near turret, allied health is low. 9:20 shows Samira escapes the enemy base after an inhib turret take and fight, setting up the ideal recall window Alan leaks by staying for more when massively ahead on gold. 10:37 shows Samira is moving through mid lane toward the enemy base with 4714 gold, while her team is regrouping on the map after a successful fight.

recordings.title: Spend the base win before the next fight
recordings.feedbackTitle: Spend the base win before the next fight
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after turning the lead into enemy-base pressure, you kept the next fight window alive while carrying a large unspent shutdown. Fix: after the base kill or tower damage, reset unless nexus is immediately finishable.
recordings.gameDetail: At 1:31 you are already farming bot safely with the wave in front, and by 3:23 you take the first bot-side fight instead of only bleeding CS; that early pressure is good. At 4:36 you correctly turn the lane win into bot tower damage with allies and minions, and at 6:56 you are still converting through bot-side structure pressure. The leak starts after the map is already cracked: at 9:47 you are walking near the enemy base with multiple enemies alive, and at 10:36 you get another kill inside the base while holding about 2497 gold. That fight works in beginner bots, but the Master-climb reason to reset is simple: unspent gold is not real power yet, and giving a shutdown before buying would erase the tempo you already earned. At 12:21 you are back on the map after spending, which is the cleaner version; the repeatable rule is base damage or a base kill first, then spend the gold unless the nexus is free right now.
recordings.eventEvidence: 1:31 shows Samira safely farming bot with wave control. 4:36 shows Samira hitting bot tower with allies and minions. 9:47 shows Samira near the enemy base while enemies are still alive. 10:36 shows a base kill while Samira is holding about 2497 gold. 12:21 shows Samira back on the map after spending.
recordings.goodThing: You did convert the lane lead into tower/base pressure instead of only chasing kills.
recordings.whyTrust: The evidence is tied to visible state: wave-first lane pressure, tower conversion, then a large unspent-gold base fight before the reset.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame review of the May 21 full-game clip: 1:31 bot wave control, 4:36 bot tower conversion, 9:47 enemy-base pressure, 10:36 base kill with about 2497 gold, and 12:21 post-spend map return.
recordings.pattern: The improvement is real: you are finding waves, towers, and the base. The next leak is staying available for one more base fight after the payout while your gold is still unspent.
recordings.diamondRule: After a base kill or structure payout, reset with big gold unless the nexus is free on the current wave.
recordings.drill: Next game, when your gold is above 2000 after a base fight, say 'nexus or reset' before clicking forward.

recordings.title: Turn the shutdown into a grouped push
recordings.feedbackTitle: Turn the shutdown into a grouped push
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after spending a huge lead, you let side fights test your shutdown before your team was set up to take tower or base. Fix: choose the safest visible payout first: group with the wave and allies, hit free structure, or reset if teammates cannot follow.
recordings.gameDetail: At 09:11 you are in base/shop after a strong 4/0/2 start, and that part is good because you actually spend instead of dragging raw gold around the map. At 10:25 you are 6/0/2 with 1285 gold in a bot-side lane fight while a shutdown has just happened, so the leak is that your biggest asset, the fed Samira shutdown, is being tested in a side lane before the team is organized to take something guaranteed. A conversion just means turning your lead into a concrete payout: tower damage, base damage, dragon/Baron setup, or a safe reset with spent gold. At 11:12 the better version appears because you are grouped mid behind allies with enemies in front; mid is better in this visible state because allies are already there, the lane points straight toward towers/base, and your team can body-block while enemies have to defend instead of collapsing from fog on a side-lane Samira. At 12:42 you are 8/0/2 mid while several allies are dead or on timers, so the correct next check is wait for allies, reset, or hold the wave until the next push is together, because clearing one more wave alone does not matter if it gives enemies your shutdown. By 13:10 and 13:24 you do convert with the team inside the enemy base, which proves the ending instinct is there; the Master-climb lesson is to make the earlier 10:25 and 12:42 windows look like that ending shape sooner.
recordings.eventEvidence: 09:11 shows Samira in shop after a 4/0/2 start with Immortal Shieldbow visible and low remaining gold. 10:25 shows Samira 6/0/2 in bot-side lane during a shutdown message with enemies and a wave still present. 11:12 shows Samira grouped mid behind allies while enemies are in front. 12:42 shows Samira 8/0/2 mid while multiple allies are dead or on timers. 13:10 to 13:24 shows the team turning the lead into enemy-base damage.
recordings.goodThing: You did spend, you did group with allies, and you did end through the base. The improvement is protecting the shutdown lead between those good moments.
recordings.whyTrust: The frames show both the good habit and the leak: you bought at 09:11 and ended with the team at 13:24, but the risky windows were the side-fight exposure at 10:25 and the alone-before-team state at 12:42.
recordings.evidence: Manual frame review of the May 21 queued auto capture: shop/spend at 09:11, side-fight shutdown state at 10:25, grouped mid pressure at 11:12, unsynced mid wave with ally death timers at 12:42, and base conversion at 13:10-13:24.
recordings.pattern: The repeated climb leak is letting a won personal lead pass through extra side fights before the next team objective is organized. The fix is not always mid; it is whichever visible route turns the lead into structure, objective setup, or a clean reset with the least shutdown exposure.
recordings.diamondRule: After a big Samira spend, do not take a fair side fight until you have first checked the guaranteed payout: group, structure, objective, or reset.
recordings.drill: When you are 4/0 or better, say payout first before accepting the next side fight.

Conversion means turning a fight win or gold lead into something that remains after the fight ends: tower damage, base damage, dragon/Baron setup, or a safe reset with spent gold. recordings.title: Base win becomes side chase
recordings.feedbackTitle: Base win becomes side chase
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after the base was cracked, you kept accepting second and third fights away from the cleanest payout. Fix: after inhibitor/base pressure, call the state out loud: free structure, body blocking structure, or reset; only fight if it directly protects one of those.
recordings.gameDetail: At 20:58 you are already in the enemy base with an open lane and enough damage to keep the push moving; that part is good objective pressure, not the mistake. At 21:14 the fight has paid you, two allies are dead, and you are holding about 2700 unspent gold, so the next Master-climb decision is to reset or regroup instead of staying available for the next collapse. At 22:27 you run down bot side alone with the base still cracked, which leaks the map win into travel time and gives enemies another respawn window. The clearest mistake is the 27:08 to 27:12 chase into the side jungle after the base is already open; by 27:56 you are back in the base brawl at low HP with multiple defenders alive, which is exactly how a won beginner-bot base sequence becomes a shutdown or stall in harder games. The lesson is not stop fighting forever; it is stop fighting unless the fight removes the body between you and the structure.

recordings.title: Overstay After Fight Win Near Inhibitor
recordings.feedbackTitle: Overstay After Fight Win Near Inhibitor
recordings.feedback: Mistake: Stayed mid and pushed into grouped enemies after breaking the inhibitor, instead of recalling to spend massive gold and play for next spawn/timer. Fix: After breaking inhibitor or mid tower, immediately recall if you are sitting on high gold and enemy spawns are coming up, instead of fighting or clearing one more wave under risk.
recordings.gameDetail: This let gold sit unspent for almost two minutes and risked feed/lethal resets. Resetting immediately after inhibitor opens lets your next push/Baron play break the base with items and safety. Around 5:44, Samira in lane near tower with two enemy champions visible; preparing to use ability; By 14:01, Samira overstays mid with massive gold after breaking inhibitor, rather than resetting for next play—shows the key lesson mistake of not recalling after objective when enemy respawns are coming. Lesson: Always convert a big objective win into a reset before enemy respawn, unless the Nexus is free.
recordings.eventEvidence: 5:44 Samira in lane near tower with two enemy champions visible; preparing to use ability.; 14:01 Samira overstays mid with massive gold after breaking inhibitor, rather than resetting for next play—shows the key lesson mistake of not recalling after objective when enemy respawns are coming.
recordings.goodThing: Executed on the inhibitor tower opportunity and converted a fight win into a structure.
recordings.whyTrust: Almost every Samira throw in this ELO comes from overstay after a won fight; resetting with a 3k+ gold spend and regrouping is the single most repeatable win conversion and prevents stall throws.
recordings.evidence: 5:44 Samira in lane near tower with two enemy champions visible; preparing to use ability.; 14:01 Samira overstays mid with massive gold after breaking inhibitor, rather than resetting for next play—shows the key lesson mistake of not recalling after objective when enemy respawns are coming.
recordings.pattern: Successfully breaks objective after a fight and keeps pushing for another wave or more, even with huge accumulated gold and enemy respawns. Delay between taking open structure and actually resetting leaves team exposed if enemy groups.
recordings.diamondRule: After breaking open inhibitor or tower and sitting at over 2000g, recall and spend before next objective, unless the Nexus is open.
recordings.drill: Next game, after breaking any inhibitor or inner tower and having over 2000g, immediately recall, spend, and only return for objective or grouped fight.

recordings.title: Reset after the bot-lane win
recordings.feedbackTitle: Reset after the bot-lane win
recordings.feedback: Mistake: after winning bot-lane pressure, you kept extending until the safe window closed. Fix: crash the wave, recall, spend, and return with item tempo instead of letting the next fight happen on old HP and old items.
recordings.gameDetail: At 3:41, bot fight win setup: Samira dives and secures a fight win in bot lane, which creates a window to crash wave and recall; by 4:32, leak consequence: Samira stays with low HP after fight instead of resetting, seen retreating into jungle while exposed to an enemy collapse; by 5:33, Samira is in the shop screen, purchasing Gluttonous Greaves with 2012 gold. Samira's inventory shows a Vampiric Scepter and Long Sword; gold spent and item build order is visible; and by 4:40, Samira attacks an enemy near red team bottom outer turret; Karthus ult beam visible. Mistake: after winning bot-lane pressure, you kept extending until the safe window closed. Fix: crash the wave, recall, spend, and return with item tempo instead of letting the next fight happen on old HP and old items. Conversion means turning a fight win or gold lead into something that remains after the fight ends: tower damage, base damage, dragon/Baron setup, or a safe reset with spent gold. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.
recordings.eventEvidence: 3:41 shows bot fight win setup: Samira dives and secures a fight win in bot lane, which creates a window to crash wave and recall. 4:32 shows leak consequence: Samira stays with low HP after fight instead of resetting, seen retreating into jungle while exposed to an enemy collapse. 5:33 shows Samira is in the shop screen, purchasing Gluttonous Greaves with 2012 gold. Samira's inventory shows a Vampiric Scepter and Long Sword; gold spent and item build order is visible. 4:40 shows Samira attacks an enemy near red team bottom outer turret; Karthus ult beam visible.
recordings.goodThing: You correctly pressured the tower and crashed the wave after fights, maximizing local advantage before the mistake.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira with a gold lead and good buy timings is unkillable in 1v1s and can snowball, but dying with shutdown gold because of greedy post-win overstays gives the enemy an easy comeback.
recordings.evidence: 3:41 Bot fight win setup: Samira dives and secures a fight win in bot lane, which creates a window to crash wave and recall.; 4:32 Leak consequence: Samira stays with low HP after fight instead of resetting, seen retreating into jungle while exposed to an enemy collapse.; 5:33 Samira is in the shop screen, purchasing Gluttonous Greaves with 2012 gold. recordings.pattern: The issue is not finding fights; it is not recognizing when the fight already bought wave control and the next value is recall tempo.
recordings.diamondRule: After a bot-lane win, crash one wave and recall unless a free structure is already being hit.
recordings.drill: After each bot-lane kill or tower push, say: crash, recall, spend.

recordings.title: Overstay and risk instead of clean reset and tempo base
recordings.feedbackTitle: Overstay and risk instead of clean reset and tempo base
recordings.feedback: Mistake: You repeatedly overstay in the enemy base for extended fights instead of resetting after breaking the inhibitor and getting picks, risking shutdown and stalling your siege. Fix: After taking the open inhibitor and winning the fight, quickly recall or disengage to reset with gold and regroup for the next push instead of forcing deep under turrets with enemies respawning.

Mistake: You repeatedly overstay in the enemy base for extended fights instead of resetting after breaking the inhibitor and getting picks, risking shutdown and stalling your siege. Fix: After taking the open inhibitor and winning the fight, quickly recall or disengage to reset with gold and regroup for the next push instead of forcing deep under turrets with enemies respawning. Conversion means turning a fight win or gold lead into something that remains after the fight ends: tower damage, base damage, dragon/Baron setup, or a safe reset with spent gold. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.

recordings.goodThing: You correctly converted the mid fight win into immediate mid turret and inhib push with team, instead of chasing deep into fog.
recordings.whyTrust: This habit is what lets Samira close games cleanly with her advantage: you force enemy teams to defend 4v5 or 3v5 with item deficit, giving you cleaner team wipes and Nexus access on the next spawn.

recordings.pattern: Alan consistently extends fights deep into base after breaking inhib, risking shutdown and burning time. Stronger teams will punish this by killing or CC-locking him for shutdown before he can participate in the next siege.
recordings.diamondRule: After inhibitor falls, always check enemy respawn timers and gold, then disengage and reset unless you have overwhelming numbers and wave to end.
recordings.drill: Next game, after any inhibitor take, immediately call reset and start recall unless you see at least two enemies dead for >20s.

recordings.feedbackTitle: Missed Recall and Overstay After Winning Lane
recordings.feedback: Mistake: Stayed bot lane with low HP and high gold after winning fight and clearing wave. Fix: Immediately recall to reset after the 4:15 kill and wave clear to spend gold and avoid dying to respawned opponents.
recordings.gameDetail: At 2:07, Samira and her support are trading with low health, chasing a low-health enemy Xayah under threat near the bottom lane brush while a fresh wave is meeting in lane, following a recent skirmish that resulted in ally deaths; by 2:52, Samira and Brand are low on health, positioned aggressively near the enemy tower against Xayah and Rakan with an enemy minion wave about to clash, creating a high-risk scenario for both sides; by 4:23, Samira and Brand are fighting a nearly dead enemy Xayah and a low-health Anivia just outside the blue team's tower, with minions approaching and both enemy champions retreating. Mistake: Stayed bot lane with low HP and high gold after winning fight and clearing wave. Fix: Immediately recall to reset after the 4:15 kill and wave clear to spend gold and avoid dying to respawned opponents. Conversion means turning a fight win or gold lead into something that remains after the fight ends: tower damage, base damage, dragon/Baron setup, or a safe reset with spent gold. This matters because a Samira lead only climbs when the next click protects the shutdown and turns the won moment into a map result before enemies get another collapse window.
recordings.eventEvidence: 2:07 shows Samira and her support are trading with low health, chasing a low-health enemy Xayah under threat near the bottom lane brush while a fresh wave is meeting in lane, following a recent skirmish that resulted in ally deaths. 2:52 shows Samira and Brand are low on health, positioned aggressively near the enemy tower against Xayah and Rakan with an enemy minion wave about to clash, creating a high-risk scenario for both sides. 4:23 shows Samira and Brand are fighting a nearly dead enemy Xayah and a low-health Anivia just outside the blue team's tower, with minions approaching and both enemy champions retreating.
recordings.goodThing: Secured a winning trade and converted for a solo kill under threat.
recordings.whyTrust: Samira is very snowball-based and falls off fast if caught after a greedy overstay; resetting with a lead is the highest-value bot lane habit past low ranks.

recordings.diamondRule: Always take the recall the moment the enemy wave is cleared and you have 1k+ gold after a fight, even if the structure is not free.
recordings.drill: Next game: recall instantly after your first post-fight wave clear with enough gold for a power buy—do not take extra waves if enemies are respawning.

recordings.feedback: Mistake: you keep playing the wave when one auto or spell kills you. Fix: give the wave and recall before the lane turns into a death timer.

At 3:27 the real mistake is clearer: Samira is still beside the bot wave at lethal health, the camera and movement are staying with minions, and the recall path has not been taken even though one clean enemy touch can turn the wave into a death timer. At 4:57 the good version is visible after the reset: Samira is back on the map with enough HP to walk forward, Q/auto from range, and choose the next trade instead of begging not to be hit. By 14:28 the team is ending with Samira alive around the base fight, which proves the champion damage is not the blocker. The blocker is staying in lane after the health bar says the next decision should be recall.

recordings.diamondRule: If you see a Teleport coming in near you and you don't have numbers, back off instantly—never greed wave or aggression with shutdown gold exposed.
recordings.drill: In your next game, back away instantly on every enemy Teleport in your lane, even if you think they might not have kill pressure—build the habit of never greeting TP plays.

nuance: You pushed an advantage up to tower pressure, showing good map presence.
The major leak was standing in melee range of the front line as a Teleport channels behind the wave.
This resulted in an instant stun and shutdown handover to an enemy frontliner.
In harder games, this mistake can lose entire tempo and hand carry gold to the enemy for free.
The next check is always to retreat far enough that the TP'd enemy cannot instantly engage you.

recordings.title: Reset after the turret falls
recordings.feedbackTitle: Reset after the turret falls
recordings.feedback: Mistake: you are dead as the red turret falls, so the objective win turns into a stagger instead of a clean reset. Fix: once the turret drops, exit with the wave or recall with the team before the next enemy bodies arrive.
recordings.gameDetail: The objective went through, so the good part is that the team got structure value; the leak is dying during or right after that conversion, which removes your ability to reset, spend, and help the next map play. The better decision after the turret falls is exit or recall, not staying in the lane long enough to become the stagger. The lesson is to make the objective win end the sequence cleanly.
recordings.goodThing: Your team broke the inhibitor turret, creating an opportunity for a powerful map reset or further pressure.
recordings.whyTrust: This death costs Samira both tempo and agency on the map, but the correct reset after a turret break directly leads to more vision and better drag/baron prep in every rank.
recordings.pattern: The structure conversion happened, but the death means the sequence did not end cleanly; the next improvement is exiting after the payout.
recordings.diamondRule: When the turret falls, the sequence is over unless the next structure is free right now.
recordings.drill: After every turret take, click back once and check recall before looking for another fight.
nuance: Good: the team still took the turret.
Leak: Samira was dead during the payout instead of resetting after it.
Consequence: the structure win becomes a stagger, so the next objective setup is weaker.
Next check: turret falls, exit or recall unless the next structure is free immediately.
recordings.reviewLimit: This short clip has one reviewable frame, so it supports the turret-death timing but not the full fight setup.
